owasp_llm_page_title: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications - API Security Reference Hub
owasp_llm_subtitle: The 10 most critical security risks for Large Language Model applications and how to mitigate them.
owasp_llm_overview_title: What is the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications?
owasp_llm_overview_desc: A ranking of the most critical security risks specific to applications that use Large Language Models (LLMs), published by OWASP. The 2025 edition reflects the rapidly evolving threat landscape as LLMs become widely deployed in production systems.
owasp_llm_llm01_overview_desc: An attacker crafts inputs that manipulate the LLM's behavior, bypassing instructions, extracting sensitive data, or triggering unintended actions. This includes both direct injection (user input) and indirect injection (via external data sources like websites or documents).
owasp_llm_llm01_risk_desc: Attackers can override system prompts, extract confidential information, execute unauthorized tool calls, or manipulate the LLM into performing harmful actions on behalf of the user.
owasp_llm_llm01_check1: Use structured message formats with clear role separation (system/user/assistant)
owasp_llm_llm01_check2: Sanitize and validate all user inputs before passing to the LLM
owasp_llm_llm01_check3: Implement output validation to detect leaked sensitive data
owasp_llm_llm01_check4: Apply the principle of least privilege to any tools or actions the LLM can invoke
owasp_llm_llm02_overview_desc: LLMs may inadvertently reveal sensitive information such as PII, API keys, proprietary business logic, or training data through their responses. This can occur through direct queries, prompt injection, or memorization of training data.
owasp_llm_llm02_risk_desc: Exposure of personal data, credentials, internal system details, or proprietary information can lead to privacy violations, unauthorized access, and compliance breaches (GDPR, HIPAA).
owasp_llm_llm02_check1: Implement PII detection and redaction on LLM outputs
owasp_llm_llm02_check2: Never include secrets, API keys, or credentials in system prompts
owasp_llm_llm02_check3: Apply data sanitization to training and fine-tuning datasets
owasp_llm_llm02_check4: Use differential privacy techniques when training on sensitive data
owasp_llm_llm03_overview_desc: LLM applications depend on third-party models, datasets, plugins, and libraries that may contain vulnerabilities, backdoors, or malicious code. Compromised pre-trained models or poisoned datasets can introduce hidden risks.
owasp_llm_llm03_check1: Verify integrity of pre-trained models with cryptographic hashes
owasp_llm_llm03_check2: Use only trusted model registries and vetted data sources
owasp_llm_llm03_check3: Pin dependency versions and audit third-party plugins
owasp_llm_llm03_check4: Implement SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) for AI components
owasp_llm_llm04_heading: 4️⃣ LLM04 - Data and Model Poisoning
owasp_llm_llm04_overview_desc: Attackers manipulate training data or fine-tuning processes to introduce biases, backdoors, or vulnerabilities into the model. This can cause the model to produce incorrect, biased, or malicious outputs under specific conditions.
owasp_llm_llm04_check1: Validate and sanitize all training data with anomaly detection
owasp_llm_llm04_check2: Maintain provenance tracking for all training datasets
owasp_llm_llm04_check3: Implement continuous monitoring for model output drift
owasp_llm_llm04_check4: Use federated learning or secure enclaves for sensitive training
owasp_llm_llm05_overview_desc: LLM outputs are used directly in downstream systems without proper sanitization. This can lead to XSS, SQL injection, command injection, or code execution when LLM-generated content is rendered, executed, or passed to other systems.
owasp_llm_llm05_risk_desc: Never use eval() or exec() on LLM outputs. Treat all LLM-generated content as untrusted user input.
owasp_llm_llm05_check1: Treat all LLM outputs as untrusted input; always sanitize and escape
owasp_llm_llm05_check2: Never use eval(), exec(), or os.system() with LLM-generated content
owasp_llm_llm05_check3: Use parameterized queries when LLM output interacts with databases
owasp_llm_llm05_check4: Apply Content Security Policy (CSP) when rendering LLM output in browsers
owasp_llm_llm06_overview_desc: An LLM-based system is granted excessive functionality, permissions, or autonomy. When combined with prompt injection or hallucinations, the model may perform destructive or unauthorized actions such as deleting data, sending emails, or making purchases.
owasp_llm_llm06_check1: Apply least privilege: only grant the minimum tools and permissions needed
owasp_llm_llm06_check2: Implement human-in-the-loop approval for high-risk actions
owasp_llm_llm06_check3: Use an allowlist for tools and functions the LLM can invoke
owasp_llm_llm06_check4: Log all tool invocations and monitor for anomalous patterns
owasp_llm_llm07_overview_desc: System prompts containing sensitive business logic, instructions, or role definitions can be extracted by users through crafted queries. Attackers can use leaked prompts to understand the system's constraints and find bypasses.
owasp_llm_llm07_check1: Never embed secrets, API keys, or passwords in system prompts
owasp_llm_llm07_check2: Implement prompt extraction detection and blocking
owasp_llm_llm07_check3: Separate business logic from prompt instructions where possible
owasp_llm_llm07_check4: Assume system prompts will eventually be leaked; design accordingly
owasp_llm_llm08_heading: 8️⃣ LLM08 - Vector and Embedding Weaknesses
owasp_llm_llm08_overview_desc: Weaknesses in how vectors and embeddings are generated, stored, or retrieved in RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems. Attackers can poison the vector database, perform embedding inversion attacks, or exploit access control gaps in knowledge retrieval.
owasp_llm_llm08_check1: Implement access control on vector database queries based on user roles
owasp_llm_llm08_check2: Validate the provenance and freshness of retrieved documents
owasp_llm_llm08_check3: Monitor for data poisoning in the vector store
owasp_llm_llm08_check4: Encrypt embeddings at rest and in transit
owasp_llm_llm09_overview_desc: LLMs can generate plausible but factually incorrect information (hallucinations). In critical applications like healthcare, legal, or financial systems, misinformation can lead to serious consequences and erode user trust.
owasp_llm_llm09_check1: Use RAG to ground responses in verified data sources
owasp_llm_llm09_check2: Display confidence scores and source citations to users
owasp_llm_llm09_check3: Add disclaimers for AI-generated content in critical domains
owasp_llm_llm09_check4: Implement human review workflows for high-stakes outputs
owasp_llm_llm10_overview_desc: LLM applications without proper resource controls can be exploited to cause excessive resource consumption. Attackers can trigger expensive API calls, generate massive token usage, or create recursive loops leading to denial of service or financial damage.
owasp_llm_llm10_check1: Set maximum token limits per request and per user session
owasp_llm_llm10_check2: Implement rate limiting and per-user quotas
owasp_llm_llm10_check3: Set daily cost budgets with automatic cutoffs
owasp_llm_llm10_check4: Monitor for recursive or looping LLM calls and set depth limits
owasp_llm_table_llm01_mitigation: Input sanitization, role separation, output validation
owasp_llm_table_llm02_mitigation: PII filtering, data sanitization, no secrets in prompts
owasp_llm_table_llm05_mitigation: Output sanitization, no eval(), parameterized queries
owasp_llm_table_llm07_mitigation: No secrets in prompts, extraction detection
owasp_llm_table_llm08_mitigation: Access control on vector DB, document validation
owasp_llm_table_llm09_mitigation: RAG grounding, confidence scores, source citations
owasp_llm_table_llm10_mitigation: Token limits, rate limiting, cost budgets
